The Fruit Salad Maker Chopper lives up to its promise of simplifying fruit prep. With sharp stainless steel blades and a sturdy base, it slices through apples, kiwis, and even tougher fruits like pineapples with minimal effort. The included collection tray keeps counters tidy, and all parts are dishwasher safe. While chunk sizes aren’t adjustable and softer fruits can get slightly mushy, for quick, consistent fruit salads, this gadget is a real timesaver. Recommended for anyone who enjoys fresh fruit but dislikes the hassle of cutting it up.

**Man Sentenced After Glass Attack at Birthday Celebration Leaves Guest Severely Injured**

A 24-year-old man has been sentenced to eight years in prison following a violent altercation at a birthday party in Manchester that left another guest with life-changing injuries. 

Ryan Baxter, of Moss Side, was found guilty of grievous bodily harm after smashing a glass bottle over the head of fellow partygoer Daniel Lee, 27, during an argument over loud music in the early hours of November 18th, 2023.

According to Manchester Crown Court, tensions began to rise when guests complained about the volume of the music at the rented apartment. Witnesses said Baxter became aggressive after Lee politely asked him to lower the volume, leading to a heated exchange. The confrontation escalated suddenly when Baxter grabbed an empty beer bottle and struck Lee, causing deep lacerations to his scalp and face.

Lee was rushed to Manchester Royal Infirmary, where he required emergency surgery to stop the bleeding and received over fifty stitches. Surgeons told the court the injuries narrowly missed Lee’s eye, and he will be permanently scarred. 

During sentencing, Judge Hannah Fitzpatrick described the attack as “a shocking act of violence fueled by alcohol and reckless disregard for human life.” She added, “Your actions that night have left a young man with injuries that will affect him for the rest of his life.”

Baxter, who pleaded not guilty, claimed he acted in self-defense, but the jury rejected his account after reviewing CCTV footage from the apartment block, which showed him initiating the attack.

In a statement read to the court, Lee said, “I still struggle to sleep at night and have flashbacks of what happened. I don’t know if I’ll ever feel safe at social events again.” He thanked friends who helped him and urged others to “think twice before letting arguments get out of control.”

Police urged partygoers to remain calm when disputes arise, emphasizing the dangers of mixing alcohol with heated tempers. Detective Inspector Laura Grant, who led the investigation, said, “This case is a stark reminder that one moment of anger can ruin lives. We hope today’s sentence brings some comfort to Mr. Lee and his family.”

Baxter will serve at least two-thirds of his sentence before becoming eligible for parole.

"Order, order," yelled the judge while furiously beating the little hammer in front of him. And perhaps order was in order because quite frankly the courtroom was chaotic at best. People had already dubbed the trial the one trial to end all trials, since many people held the accused responsible for everything wrong in this world even their own lesser evils. And with such an important trial it was no wonder the building was absolutely swamped with people. Everyone had come to witness the impending downfall of the one. He was seated fairly comfortably it seemed. His hooves crossed, hair combed neatly backwards between his horns. It was convenient that the chairs in this courtroom had open backs because it allowed him to hang his tail through the back. As he played with his little goatee I glanced at my notepad and picked up my pen. "Your honor. My client wishes to plead not guilty to all but one of the charges. He will freely admit that he indeed double-parked on Friday the thirteenth in anticipation of a meeting with Bin Laden." I straightened my tie and tried my best to not glance away from the judge's piercing eyes. "However, this meeting was purely personal and no plans of any sort were made. He did not give instructions on the exact floors that needed to be hit to structurally damage the towers in order for them to collapse on their own. My client wishes to express that even the notion of this is preposterous. In fact the one you should be suing is God since he was sleeping on the job and grossly negligent." The room had gone silent. Nobody had dared to say this in court up until now. And now, with evil present, it was evil itself that confronted us with the harsh truth. It was not evil that ruled these two decades, it was lack of good. Lack of courage, lack of compassion, lack of empathy, lack of love. We had aided and abetted evil by withholding goodness. "I would like to say a few words," a low, raspy voice behind me said. I turned around and mouthed to my client to sit down. He would not listen. "Thank you, John. You're a great lawyer but please be seated. There are a few words I would like to say. I, most of all people, know what it is like to be confronted by the results of your actions. I have gone through the ages, killing, ravaging, plundering. I will admit to these crimes and more and will accept appropriate punishment. However, as unbelievable as it may seem to you I have actually not interfered in about 500 years. I decided to let people make their own destiny. And now that you have created this destiny you still seek someone to blame. I will let you in on a secret; you should sue God because of negligence. He didn't water the plants of compassion and love. And you know what? Here's the next secret, there is no God. He exists within each and every one of you. If you can't find empathy, compassion or love in this world don't look further than your own heart because it starts with you. Now crucify me, or do whatever it is your heart compels you to do. My mind has been purer than those who blame me now. I'm ready to return." With a swift arm movement the sound of the hammer on wood echoed through the courtroom. "Guilty, let's shoot this asshole."

The leatherback sea turtle population in the Pacific Ocean, which was once the largest and most stable group of the species worldwide, has plummeted by more than 90% since 1980, with fewer than 2,300 adult females estimated to remain. Today, a significant portion of the remaining Pacific leatherbacks nest primarily on Playa Grande, Playa Ventanas, and Playa Langosta along Costa Rica’s northern Pacific coast. During nesting season, female leatherbacks use their powerful rear flippers to excavate holes in the sand and lay an average of 80 rubbery, ping-pong-ball-shaped eggs per clutch, returning to nest up to 12 times in a single season, with 9-15 days between nesting events. After about 60-70 days of incubation, the vulnerable hatchlings emerge, facing long odds of survival.

Both adult leatherbacks and their hatchlings face numerous threats, including increasing sand temperatures and nest inundation due to climate change, entanglement and injury from commercial fishing gear such as longlines and gillnets, collisions with boats, coastal development, light pollution, and illegal poaching of eggs for human consumption. Population declines have been especially severe in Pacific nesting sites due to these overlapping hazards. To develop effective conservation strategies and halt their slide toward extinction, researchers must collect detailed data on leatherback behavior during nesting, physiological adaptation to variable environments, genetic diversity in declining populations, reproductive success rates, and critical migration routes spanning thousands of miles across the Pacific.

Supporting this research means hands-on work in the field: at night when leatherbacks haul onto the beach to nest, you’ll join scientists to attach satellite or radio transmitters to large females to monitor their local beach movements and track transoceanic migrations. You’ll also help carefully transfer eggs from exposed or flood-prone nests—often just meters from the high tide line—into protected hatcheries, documenting each clutch and monitoring for predation or fungal threats. With recent studies estimating that without intervention, the Eastern Pacific leatherback could disappear within the next 10-20 years, every hatchling has heightened significance and every adult is vital to rebuilding the population.

Its been three months since she fell overboard. I look at the picture of us together and I can almost feel Claire's hand. She always loved sunsets. Eight countries without stopping longer than two days in each place. Its exhausting but essential so I can meet people without them guessing I'm travelling alone. "We've gone our separate ways for a few days" I tell them. Claire has looked forward to this trip for two years. I held the crippled piece of paper in my hand with twenty lines crossed off. Only one remains. The last wish. I've started thinking about it as her resting place. I'm probably mentally disturbed. I passed the camera to a foreign tourist, they're the best. Not too many questions. Snap, snap, snap. "Just one more please, it has to be perfect". They didn't understand but continued to take pictures as long as I looked animated. They laughed when handing back the camera. Probably thought it was a joke. A pretend friend or something. Ha, yeah. Real funny. Back at the hotel I crossed the last line off the list after hours of editing. Those big green eyes, huge smile and her personality radiating from the image. One big wave took it all away as she slipped into the dark. In trying to bring her closer to me I'll be ostracising our family and friends. They wont understand. But I'm not ready to move on. Not yet. Sipping my drink I write a letter on the back of the crumpled paper to no one in particular about my thoughts. It makes the situation feel better. I could leave it a mystery but I have to tell my story. I title the letter a 'wave and a blade'. In the last seconds I realise I've got more to add to the letter but the blood will mark the paper. Its just better to sit back in the bath and wait for the end. As I feel sleepy I know no one will add photos of me to pictures apart from the newspapers. As I look at the photos in my hand I know our trip was complete. Together.

No More Running

Elara stumbled, roots like grasping fingers. Her lungs burned, a fire that had been raging for weeks, or was it months? The forest was a blur of greens and browns, each shadow a potential threat. She didn't know *what* she was running from anymore, just the relentless *need* to put distance between herself and… everything. Her past, her failures, the ghost of a child's laughter that haunted her quietest moments.

She fell, scraping her knee on a sharp stone. The pain was dull compared to the ache in her soul. Lying there, cheek pressed against damp earth, a thought, clear and sharp like ice, pierced the fog of exhaustion: *Why?*

Why was she still running? What did she hope to find at the end of this endless flight? Another place to hide, another patch of ground to scar with her fear?

Slowly, deliberately, she pushed herself up. Her muscles screamed in protest, but a new kind of strength, cold and steady, was rising within her. She looked back the way she came, into the deepening shadows of the woods. There was nothing there but the rustling of leaves, the whisper of the wind. Or was it the sound of her own heart, finally beating a different rhythm?

She stood tall, as tall as her worn frame allowed. The fear was still a tremor in her hands, but it no longer commanded her. Her gaze swept over the trees, then up to the fading light of the sky. A sense of peace, stark and unexpected, settled over her.

No More Running.

It wasn't a surrender. It was a choice. A turning. She reached into her tattered satchel, her fingers closing around the cold, smooth stone she’d carried for years – a child’s river stone, smooth from countless washes. She held it tight. She would build. She would face. She would simply *be*. The forest was no longer a maze to escape, but a place to stand.

Nick Clegg yesterday announced plans for an £8 billion tax hike after the election, as he laid the groundwork for a coalition with Labour. In a significant shift towards Labour’s position, Mr Clegg said the Lib Dems would pursue a higher ratio of tax rises to spending cuts if they find themselves still in power again after the election. He said this would involve ‘at least’ £8 billion in tax rises, more than half of which have yet to be set out. Scroll down for video . In a significant shift towards Labour’s position, Nick Clegg (left) said the Lib Dems would pursue a higher ratio of tax rises to spending cuts if they find themselves still in power again after the election. Right: Ed Miliband . David Cameron condemned the move, saying it would hit millions of ordinary families. But the shift opens the door to a potential post-election deal with Ed Miliband, who is also expected to raise taxes and reduce the pace of spending cuts if he wins power in May. The coalition has pursued a deficit reduction programme comprising 80 per cent spending and welfare cuts and 20 per cent tax rises. But the new Lib Dem plans will see that shift to 60 per cent cuts and 40 per cent tax rises after the election. The Conservatives want to clear the remaining budget deficit entirely through cuts to spending and benefits. Mr Clegg yesterday dismissed the Tory plans as ‘implausible and undeliverable’. Speaking at the Shangri La Hotel on the 52nd floor of the Shard building in London, Mr Clegg said his party’s ‘sensible, balanced approach would bring an end to the era of austerity in three years’ time while protecting public services and the most vulnerable in society. ‘Austerity should only be a means to an end - and the end is in sight,’ he said. Mr Clegg insisted that the planned tax rises would not hit those on low and middle incomes, and would not involve a rise in the headline rates of income tax, National Insurance or VAT. But speaking at a campaign event in Leeds, Mr Cameron dismissed the Lib Dem proposals, warning that they would hit ‘hard-working people’. ‘I don’t think it is right to have a plan that involves really quite substantial tax increases that would hit hard working people,” he said. ‘I think that what we need now is a plan that the Conservative Party has set out which is to eradicate the remaining deficit, to start putting money aside during years of growth for a rainy day.’ Mr Clegg (pictured) said his party’s ‘sensible, balanced approach would bring an end to the era of austerity in three years’ time while protecting public services and the most vulnerable in society . The deputy Prime Minister was speaking at the Shangri La Hotel on the 52nd floor of the Shard in London . The Lib Dem shift came as a new analysis found the party now shares almost 100 policies, fuelling speculation that they are plotting a post-election Coalition. Agreement over major issues such as the mansion tax, votes at 16 and a green light for further government borrowing would make it easier for Mr Miliband and Mr Clegg to come to a deal in May. A commitment to implement in full the draconian recommendations of the Leveson Report into press regulation is also shared between the two leaders’ parties – as is a commitment to sex and relationship education in schools. The analysis by the Fabian Society and Centre Forum, two think tanks, suggests that agreement between Labour and the Lib Dems may be significantly easier than between the Tories and the Lib Dems. The two organisations, which are close to the respective parties, has identified 97 areas of agreement in their joint report, ‘Common Ground?’. These include fiscal rules which permit the government to borrow billions more, a mansion tax for properties over £2million, decarbonising the power sector by 2030, and major devolution of power and money to English councils. Other shared priorities include more free childcare for children under five, greater control over free schools and academies, the construction of at least 200,000 new homes a year, and restrictions on access to some benefits for EU migrants while supporting international students. The two parties also agree on a higher minimum wage, withdrawal of the winter fuel payment from the richest pensioners, votes at 16 and a commitment that the UK should ‘remain a committed member of the EU’ and of the European Convention on Human Rights.

- Romanticism and Its Discontents by Anita Brookner
Viking, 208 pp, £25.00, September 2000, ISBN 0 670 89212 2
Trying to define Romanticism has always been a typically Romantic activity, especially in France. The word romantisme first appeared in the year of Napoleon’s coronation (1804) and soon began to acquire a large retinue of definitions. Mme de Staël associated it with the misty, melancholy North and declared Romanticism to be primarily an effect of climate. Victor Hugo and his followers allied it to the vanished monarchy, then to the departed Napoleon, and finally to ‘liberalism in art’. Stendhal and Baudelaire produced more durable definitions by linking it to the present. For Stendhal, ‘Romanticism is the art of offering people the literary works which, in the present state of their habits and beliefs, are likely to give them the greatest possible pleasure. Classicism, by contrast, offers them the literature which gave the greatest possible pleasure to their great-grandfathers.’ For Baudelaire, ‘whoever says Romanticism, says modern art.’
In her discreetly original study of Romanticism and Its Discontents, Anita Brookner quotes Alfred de Musset’s satire, Lettres de Dupuis et Cotonet (1836-37). Dupuis and Cotonet are two literal-minded provincials who spend 12 years trying to find out what Romanticism is. At first, it seems to refer to plays which ignore the Classical unities – except in the provinces, where ‘Romantic’ simply means ‘absurd’. But then they discover that there are also Romantic novels, poems, epics and even single lines of verse. In 1827, Hugo’s Préface de Cromwell reveals Romanticism to be a combination of tragedy and comedy. But it also seems to mean the imitation of the Germans, the English and the Spanish. By the mid-1830s, it has something to do with socialism and a refusal to shave or to do one’s National Guard duty. Eventually, they decide that Romanticism is distinguished from Classicism by the use of a lot of vague, colourful adjectives.
If Dupuis and Cotonet had lived for another century and a half, they would have reached previously unsuspected depths of confusion. According to Hans Eichner, it was not until the late 1970s that most scholars finally realised ‘that “Romanticism” is not a technical term like “propanol” or “cosine”, invented to name a precise concept . . . and that any definition capable of encompassing Keats’s sonnet “To Sleep”, Novalis’s Heinrich von Ofterdingen and Hugo’s Hernani must be so broad as to be meaningless.’ The desire for unattainable precision and curious universal truths is also a Romantic trait, and it may be that some of the more ambitious forms of modern literary criticism will come to be seen as a late flowering of the Romantic spirit.
When the various definitions are brought together, they tend to suggest that the Romantics were writers and artists who knew that they were doing something radically different from their fathers but were unable to say precisely what it was. Rimbaud, who wrote his only extended piece of literary history at the age of 16, was probably closer to a good general definition than most professional critics when he identified self-ignorance as the crucial Romantic trait: ‘Romanticism has never been properly judged. Who would have judged it? The critics!? The Romantics, who show so clearly that the song is very rarely the work, which is to say the thought of the singer, sung and understood?’

I came inside and dropped my car keys on the table in the foyer. After fighting both traffic and my company's byzantine expense report process all day, I just wanted to relax with a beer and watch some TV. I flicked on the set in the middle of a commercial break. 

"It's cold here in this village," began the TV announcer in a voice and manner of over-seriousness, "but Jessica has no coat." The sharp display of the plasma screen shifted to show an image of a young, pale-skinned girl. She couldn't have been more than eight years old, and she was dressed in a faded dashiki that was worn at the edges. As the music played, the screen alternated between shots of Jessica looking into the camera with glossy, blue eyes and shots of her walking slowly along a cracked sidewalk in a ruined cityscape. 

"She'll spend the winter looking for scraps of food in these abandoned factories while her brothers hunt rats and squirrels." On screen, Jessica lay curled in a ball, wrapping her arms around her knees on a concrete floor. "Life here is hard for Jessica. Her mother was killed when she was just eight years old. Now her father takes care of her and her six siblings. Hunger is a constant struggle." 

For the first time, the announcer showed himself on screen. His face was adorned by a white beard, and he wore a thick, corduroy jacket. He held Jessica's hand as the two of them walked along a raggedy, brick street. "For just a few kip per month, the Ukrainian Children's Fund—and you—can help a child just like Jessica make it through another winter in this war-torn part of North America." The screen cut to an image of a more robust-looking Jessica sitting at an old-looking desk and writing a letter. "We'll use your money to feed your adopted American child, give her warm clothes, and teach her how to read and write." 

I couldn't help myself. My day at the office was stressful, but these commercials always have a way of putting things in perspective. I grabbed my cell phone and gave the operator the number of my Laotian Express Card.

I peer down the ether to the leader of my people. Through the shimmering I watch. He is sitting, hopefully pondering how to spread the Word of our faith. What exactly that Word entails is anyone's guess. It's a common misconception that a god has any idea what is going on. The popular ones, yes, they can sometimes guess what the weather will be like that day, or the score of the upcoming Super Bowl, but I'm beginning to think it's all luck. Have a thousand gods all think they know the future and one or two will. It's a numbers game. Anyways, the leader of my flock, Steve, is starting to look like he is doing nothing at all at the moment. Doesn't he realize that the recruiting numbers for this quarter are down? Perhaps not- he doesn't have access to the data that I have. We pulled in a few new members last week but they left after their high wore off. The grandiose promises Steve made during the group's ego death didn't seem so exciting the next morning. "Steve," I said, without saying. Steve dropped his Big Gulp on the orange shag carpet. "What," said Steve, swiping at the mess with a wet towel. "We need to talk." I spun the volume dial up and increased the bass. Steve flicked at the charging component of his e-bong. "About what?" he said, absently. "About the future of our people." "What about us?" he was now whacking the bong with force. I ignore Jesus smirking my way and turn away from him. "Jesus, *again*?" The favorite son of Big God is now weeping with laughter next to me. "No, not Jesus, *Blortmort*." Steve leaned back in his chair and exhaled a huge cloud which spread along the stained stucco ceiling. "Yeah, yeah. I have a few things in the works. But you're going to need to help me out. How about a miracle or two during the recruitment sessions?" "It's not that simple, Steve. My power is derived from the belief in me. Without a following, there isn't much I can do. Plus, it's not hard for you to impress the stoners that usually show up to these things. Do a card trick or something and say that I did it." "Blortmort, I need to tell you something. I'm thinking about switching sides. I mean, I really appreciate all the free weed and whatnot, but I'm not identifying with your message anymore." "What's wrong with my message?" "Well, what is it?" "My message? It's hard to explain to a mere mortal." "See there you go, always talking down to me. I'm supposed to be a prophet. Get in on the ground floor, you told me. But you're not giving me anything to work with. How about turning Listerine strips to LSD or something? Anything. I'm starting to think you don't have any powers at all." "I can give you more weed...It's all up from here...You will be remembered forever if you stick it out," I rattled off my Hail Mary's. "I've already decided. I'm a Buddhist now. Take care," he said, throwing on his oversized headphones. In the stall two over from me, Buddha's gut grew with an imperceptible jiggle and the screen showing his number of followers grew by one. He smiled and winked my way. Perhaps I should go after the hipsters. Tell them that they can claim to be a follower before it was cool.

The Royal Navy has a longstanding tradition that says, "The Captain must go down with the ship". This is not meant to be taken literally, mind you; you're expected to make sure that you are the last man off, that everything practical has been done to save the ship and that every survivor has reached the lifeboats before you yourself abandon her, but you don't have to commit honourable suicide or anything like that. I've heard it said that when Imperial Japan began studying the habits of highly effective naval powers, this particular nuance was lost in translation and ship's captains took to doing things like lashing themselves to the ship's compass. This practice was moderately successful in weeding out the incompetent, but it also had an unfortunate tendency to kill off skilled captains who were simply unlucky, and such things are strongly discouraged in modern navies. Nevertheless, as I watch my command slip beneath the waves in the embrace of the brand-new and very expensive cruise liner we inadvertently rammed while several news helicopters circle above the waves and said cruise liner's captain stands in a lifeboat telling me *exactly* what he thinks of my seamanship, I can't help but feel the Japanese might have been on to something. I mean, *anything*'s got to be better than explaining this fiasco to the Board of Inquiry.

This is a poorly organized work that lacks for an atlas the purports to discuss Canadian military history, lacks Canadian context in many of the narratives and, worst of all, includes maps that are small and poorly drawn. Several key elements are glossed over or simply missed (for example, there is no map of Kosovo, although it is mentioned in the title). Unfortunately shoddy research and poor map design has robbed us of what should have been an important and very useful book.

**Patek Philippe CEO Thierry Stern Prioritizes Tradition and Integrity Over Short-Term Gains**

Geneva, June 14, 2024 — In an era where luxury brands often chase rapid growth and record-breaking profits, Patek Philippe's President Thierry Stern is steadfast in his commitment to tradition and the long-term integrity of the storied Swiss watchmaker.

Stern, who has helmed the renowned family-owned Maison since 2009, emphasized this philosophy during recent interviews and public statements. “For Patek Philippe, it’s not about producing as many watches as possible or maximizing short-term revenues,” Stern asserted. “Our true focus is on preserving the values and craftsmanship that have defined the brand for generations.”

Founded in 1839, Patek Philippe has long set itself apart in the horological world, not just for its technical innovation, but also for its exclusivity. Even with surging global demand, Stern has resisted calls to significantly increase production, adhering instead to a limit of about 70,000 timepieces annually. “Quality always comes first,” he said, noting that scaling up too quickly could jeopardize the brand’s hard-earned reputation.

In contrast to competitors who have embraced aggressive marketing and expansion strategies, Stern has championed the slow, deliberate training of new watchmakers and artisans. “Each watch is a promise—an object that will often outlast its first owner,” he remarked. “It’s our duty to make sure that promise is honored for decades, even centuries.”

This conservative approach extends to Patek Philippe’s policies on retail and the secondary market. The brand rigorously vets its authorized dealers and has taken steps to reduce speculative flipping, sometimes refusing to service timepieces sold through unauthorized channels. “When someone buys a Patek Philippe, they become a custodian of our history,” Stern reflected. “We want our watches to go to collectors who genuinely value them, not speculators just looking for profit.”

Stern’s dedication has not only maintained Patek Philippe’s allure among collectors, but also helped it weather turbulent economic times. While luxury markets have faced volatility, the brand’s approach has fostered unwavering loyalty. “We’re not aiming to be the biggest, but the best,” Stern reiterated. “It’s a strategy that may not deliver the highest immediate returns, but it ensures that Patek Philippe will thrive for generations to come.”

For Stern, safeguarding the company’s legacy is not simply a business decision, but a family responsibility. “My father instilled in me that our first duty is to protect the brand—its traditions, values, and the trust of our customers above all else.”

In the world of luxury horology, that commitment may be Patek Philippe’s most valuable asset.

Kroger is recalling select pet food in 19 states due to possible aflatoxin contamination, a toxin that can harm animals. The recall affects specific bags of Pet Pride Cat Food, Pet Pride Kitten Food, Old Yeller Chunk Dog Food, Kroger Value Cat Food, and Kroger Value Chunk Food. Kroger urges customers to contact a veterinarian immediately if pets show signs like sluggishness, loss of appetite, yellow eyes or gums, severe bleeding, or diarrhea. Aflatoxin is a fungal toxin that can contaminate crops like maize, according to the CDC. Customers can use Kroger’s Recall Notification system to check if they bought recalled products, most of which expire October 23 or 24, 2011. The recall applies to Kroger stores in Alabama, Arkansas, Georgia, Illinois, Indiana, Kansas, Kentucky, Louisiana, Michigan, Mississippi, Missouri, Nebraska, North Carolina, Ohio, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, Virginia, and West Virginia.

As technology increases, along with company and organizational growth, the demand for filling STEM jobs will see a direct correlation. That is to say, the projected growth for STEM employment is expected to be around 13 percent between 2012 and 2022. This is to exceed market expectations for other jobs during that time by about two percent (“STEM101: Intro to Tomorrow’s Jobs,” 2014).
Considering the potential impact these new STEM careers will have on the global economy is massive; these jobs will range from new variations on programming and engineering to an innovative take on data analysis and development.
One of the new fields emerging in STEM is robotic engineering (Pertit, n.d.). While a student may elect to take the traditional route of mechanical or electrical engineering in college, the challenges awaiting in robotics are far from ordinary. Typically, this area has been relegated to manufacturing (specifically needed to create programmable arms), but as robots continue to be modified and reinvented, their usefulness has now expanded to solving problems within the medical and agricultural fields. Interestingly enough, those entering this field may also have the possibility to save lives in inventing robots to complete tasks that have historically proven “too hazardous to be performed by people” (“Robotics engineer,” n.d.).
However, robotic engineers are not the only ones that have the potential to save lives. Bioengineers have been in the process of developing ways for those needing organ transplants to regenerate those organs using their own cells. By doing this, not only will the wait time for organs be eliminated, but the tragedies associated with not finding donors in time will prospectively decrease. This rising group of engineers is known as the “organ designers”. In applying mathematical models and statistics, organ designers are able to build “organs, limbs, knees, hips, heart valves and dental implants to replace lost function; others are growing living tissues to replace failing organs” (“Designing a career in biomedical engineering,” 2003). For students interested in this field, having a rich and varied background in both science (biology) and math is encouraged.
Another interesting, burgeoning field in the sciences is related to water conservation. Because “only 2% of the world’s water is fresh” (coupled with climate changes and shifting weather patterns), the possibility of limited access to fresh water is very real (Scott, 2014). With this in mind, water harvesters endeavor to find new, dynamic ways of automating filtering, which can provide for an accelerated conversion process. This ongoing effort to improve automation holds significant value in the face of natural disasters. Water harvesters typically have a strong understanding of environmental science and safe environmental practices (Peritt).
Meeting the demands of these challenges continues to be of critical importance. From finding ways to improve our quality of life to building systems that alleviate global problems, the capacity of STEM-related careers will only be known as history unfolds. PCS Edventures offers a wide variety of curriculum and products that enables students to understand how to approach these concerns. In equipping students with the basic understanding of concepts foundational to STEM, they can have an early start in creating and imagining the future solutions to today’s problems.

It's always so loud in here, even when it's not that busy. This time when we were here, there were plenty of open tables, but when they seated other customers, we were all grouped in a tight cluster instead of being spread out. I also don't feel that the portion sizes are equal to what I paid for. While our meals were prepared as ordered and the server was friendly and personable, he could have done a better job with drink refills. I would return, but it wouldn't be my first choice.

A 47-year-old Canadian woman went missing from a Celebration Cruise Line ship while it was traveling from Grand Bahama Island to Florida. Her boyfriend last saw her at a gift shop on board around 1am Wednesday before he went to the casino and then returned to their cabin. When he woke up and realized she hadn’t returned, he reported her missing to the crew at 8am. Authorities and the U.S. Coast Guard are searching an area of 900 miles for her. The cruise line provided investigators with the ship’s route and searched the ship twice, but she has not been found. The woman was confirmed to have boarded the ship in Grand Bahama, as all passengers use swipe cards to get on and off. The ship, which holds 2,000 people, docked at Port of Palm Beach in Florida and is scheduled to return to Grand Bahama as usual.

Title: Predation of the newly invasive pest Megacopta cribraria (F.) (Hemiptera: Plataspidae) in soybean habitats adjacent to cotton by a complex of predators Authors
Submitted to: Journal of Economic Entomology
Publication Type: Peer Reviewed Journal
Publication Acceptance Date: March 31, 2014
Publication Date: May 1, 2014
Citation: Greenstone, M.H., Tillman, P.G., Hu, J.S. 2014. Predation of the newly invasive pest Megacopta cribraria (F.) (Hemiptera: Plataspidae) in soybean habitats adjacent to cotton by a complex of predators. Journal of Economic Entomology. 107:947-954. Interpretive Summary: The kudzu bug is a newly-invasive insect feeding primarily on kudzu, but it also is a pest in soybean in the southeastern United States, where it constitutes a serious threat to crop production. Our main objective in this research was to identify predators of the kudzu bug by detecting DNA of this bug in the guts of predatory insects and spiders collected from soybean and adjacent cotton. We also wished to determine whether these predators included other pest insects in the two crops in their diets. Eight native arthropod predators, including two big-eyed bugs, a pirate bug, a predatory stink bug, the leafhopper assassin bug, a lady beetle, and two species of lynx spider, contained DNA remains of the kudzu bug in their guts; one exotic predator, the red imported fire ant, also tested positive for kudzu bug DNA. We found two species of plant-feeding stink bugs exclusively on soybean, and another only on cotton. Many of the collected predators had DNA of both the kudzu bug and one or more of these plant-feeding stinkbugs in their guts, indicating that these predators likely migrate between cotton and soybean. Therefore, establishing cotton “refuges” for predators adjacent to soybean could enhance their effectiveness as biological control agents against kudzu bug. These results are of interest to soybean producers, pest management consultants, and scientists.
Technical Abstract: The kudzu bug, Megacopta cribraria (F.) (Hemiptera: Plataspidae), is a newly-invasive exotic insect found primarily on kudzu, but also on soybean, in the southeastern United States. We used molecular gut-content analysis to document predation on this pest by insects and spiders in soybean; and to detect remains of crop-specific alternative prey in predators’ guts as markers of predator migration between soybean and adjacent cotton. Megacopta cribraria was found only on soybean. Eight native generalist predators in both crops screened positive by specific PCR for DNA of the pest: Geocoris punctipes (Say), Geocoris uliginosus (Say), Orius insidiosus (Say), Podisus maculiventris (Say), Hippodamia convergens Guérin-Méneville, Zelus renardii (Kolenati), Oxyopes salticus Hentz, and Peucetia viridans (Hentz); a ninth predator, the exotic Solenopsis invicta Buren, also screened positive for M. cribraria DNA. Two plant-feeding pentatomid species, Piezodorus guildinii (Westwood) and Thyanta custator (F.), were found exclusively on soybean, and another, Euschistus tristigmus (Say), was specific to cotton. Predation on a combination of M. cribraria and one or more of the three stink bug species by significant numbers of predators in soybean and adjacent cotton revealed that these predators likely dispersed between the two crops. This suggests that establishment of refugia for these predators might enhance their effectiveness against M. cribraria on soybean. This is the first report of predation in the field on this exotic pest in North America.

I know a lot of people who suffer from environmental allergies and they are tired of taking medications and want to enjoy life especially during times of high pollen counts. There are a few reasons for this. I want to discuss one of the primary possibilities that most people don’t think about regarding seasonal allergies.
Interestingly enough, many environmental allergies that we suffer from are actually secondary allergies being driven by primary allergic responses, specifically to foods. I have seen many times in my practice that when basic food allergies are corrected, environmental sensitivities typically vanish (there are exemptions to every rule). Why is this so? If your body is “under attack” so to speak with food that you might be eating every day, this taxes your immune system and makes your body susceptible for other “allergens” that many people aren’t affected by on a daily basis. Again, this isn’t the only reason but if you are suffering from this, it might be worth a try. Always consult with a doctor first before starting any new regimen.
The basic four food allergies are wheat, milk, corn and soy (followed by beef, tomato, peanut, chocolate). The hypo-antigenic diet consists of eliminating these eight foods. Usually within three days of elimination, chronic inflammation reduces therefore potentially reducing or eliminating the allergic response. Although change will continue for months after eliminating a basic food allergy, change can be felt immediately, sometimes as increased energy, clarity of mind, digestive wellness, and a reduction of symptoms.
I also have patients remove these items then re-introduce only one item after two weeks. Upon reintroduction of an eliminated food allergy one may notice an immediate inflammatory response such as a rash, itching or burning eyes. At times I have had a patient get a cold or a feeling of a virus after re-introducing. If no reaction to the first food after 3 or 4 days, add back another food and see if there is a reaction and so on. If you do have a reaction after reintroduction, it’s best to wait another two weeks to fully clear out the first allergen then add back another food. It’s one thing to tell a patient not to have something, but if they remove it, add it back in and they feel bad, they have a much clearer understanding of why they shouldn’t eat a particular food.
Basic food allergies are oftentimes the result of genetic predispositions, blood types and unique metabolism. Reducing inflammation will lengthen our lives, reduce discomfort, especially of the musculo-skeletal nature, and spare the immune system to be active in ways that protect us from more important issues than chronic inflammation.
You can contact Dr. Scott Jurica, MS, DC, PAK, ACN, at his website, www.drscottjurica.com, or at his office, 830-833-0889.

Kobe beef steak costing £150 for 180g will soon be available in London at Martin Williams' new restaurant . The former managing director of Gaucho is set to open the first of his three establishments M Grill next month on Threadneedle Street in the City. The international grill restaurant specialises in the best steak cuts from across the world and offers single cuts from six selected countries - Argentina, France, South Africa, Japan, Australia, and the US - with prices ranging from £18 to £149. Steaking a claim: Martin Williams' M Grill will be serving Kobe beef steaks which cost £150 for 180g . The 38-year-old plans to open a second M in the West End this time next year, with a third planned for 2016. Mr Williams told MailOnline: 'I was very keen to not shy away from Kobe because of its price. When I was in Tokyo last month, I became obsessed by the unique quality and flavour. 'For M GRILL we selected six countries in the world that breed the best beef, then chose a single cut from each. 'The beef offering at M Grill ranges from Onglet from France at £18 and rump from Argentina at £19 to Blackmoor Australian Wagyu Sirloin at £130 and Kobe fillet at £150. 'We are the United Nations of steak! 'My family has a home in France, where I have become an Onglet Addict. It isn't about price, it’s about quality. Elite beef: Kobe's high fat content ensures excellent marbling and a buttery, decadent texture . Mr Williams is 'incredibly excited' about the November opening of his new venture M Grill . 'Kobe is phenomenally expensive and phenomenally rare,' he also said to The Standard. Mr Williams' second restaurant Raw shares the same 15,000sqft site as Grill. The space will house a wine bar, a 'secret den for City boys' and sleeping pods as well as the two 100-cover restaurants. The former Gaucho boss - who worked in the famous steakhouse chain for over nine years - is 'incredibly excited' about the November opening and has assembled a 'dream team' to ensure his venture is a success. 'The amount of bookings that we have received, since opening our phone lines last week has been phenomenal,' he said. 'I have handpicked an amazing team from London’s top restaurants to join me in creating M. 'To have secured my Head Chef from Chiltern Fire House and Restaurant Director from Cecconi’s is fantastic. I have a "Dream Team" and can’t wait to take hospitality to a new level with them.' Mr Williams is the former managing director of Gaucho, a steak chain with 17 restaurants worldwide . Costly cattle: Kobe beef refers specifically to cuts of meat taken from the Tajima breed of cow (pictured) Mr Williams (third from left) with his 'Dream Team' (from left to right) : Katie Watkins, Andre Mannini, Francesco Cappucci, Zack Chariliou, Julie Reynaud,  Michael Reid and Jarad McCarroll, formerly of Chiltern Firehouse . Japanese beef - which was banned for 13-years after the BSE scare - has only recently been reintroduced to London. Kobe is prized for its superb marbling, buttery texture and high percentage of fat (it can contain up to 25 per cent fat). Kobe beef refers specifically to meat taken from the Tajima breed of cow, which are rumoured to be given daily massages to tenderise their meat.

I recently took my car in for a detailing service, expecting it to come back spotless and refreshed. Unfortunately, the experience was disappointing. While the exterior was washed, I noticed several spots were still dirty, and the tires hadn’t been properly cleaned. Inside, the vacuuming was rushed, leaving crumbs in the seats and dust on the dashboard. Additionally, the windows were streaky and not polished as promised in their package. Overall, I felt the service did not match the price or the expectations set by their advertising. I would not recommend this detailing service and will be looking elsewhere in the future.

We went to eat at the bar around 5:50 pm Wednesday. It was 20 min before we stopped a waitress who was walking  by our table. She said I'll get your server. It was almost 15 minutes before the bartender came to take our order. She brought us drinks 10 - 12 min later but still hadn't taken our order. She came back to take our order 10 minutes later. 20 minutes later she came by and said she was going to bring us some rolls. 10 min later we got the rolls... 15 min later we got our food. It was good except the garlic mashed potatoes were kind of like a soup or something. The servers were polite . They need more waitresses. The menu prices are a lot higher and the servings are a lot smaller than they used to be. Same situation 2 weeks ago * We finally walked out after we'd been there a half hour and had ordered drinks 15 minutes before and we decided that's long enough and left without getting our drinks or paying.
